question: does the latency-screened pruned graft (r=0.60 the only fp16 winner) keep usable accuracy?
protocol: prune-then-TRAIN: fresh graft (OFA-ImageNet backbone + warm gate head), CP 6.1 DepGraph (uniform per-layer, group-L2, one-shot, round_to=16), BN re-estimate, 100-epoch bare-AdamW, seed 0; dense_scaling-comparable; latencies = screen's measured Nano numbers (weight-independent)
verdict: Retention far exceeds the crater-prior (-2.5 pts at 64% sparsity, -8.2 at 84%; mAP50 >= 0.896) and accuracy-per-param is mid-pack (r40 0.816 edges dense w13 0.813 at ~1M params despite the recipe handicap). But against the measured cross-family frontier both rungs are strictly dominated (gap ~+7-8 pts at matched latency): the graft's deficit is ms-per-param (memory-bound depthwise), and pruning to latency-parity cannot buy it back. Screen prior CONFIRMED. Caveat: floor pruner config (uniform/magnitude/one-shot/no-KD) -> these are lower bounds; the pruning-as-search program upgrades the technique symmetrically.
